paper_title: Enhancing EPA Content in an Arctic Diatom: A Factorial Design Study to Evaluate Interactive Effects of Growth Factors

methods.description: Setting up and growing batch cultures of A. septentrionalis under different conditions (salinity, irradiance, growth phase) for experimental analysis. Includes pre-culture acclimation and daily/periodic sampling for various analyses.

steps.description: Two sterile and nutrient enriched media (Walne, 1970) were prepared with aged SW (salinity of 35) and respective dilutions with distilled water (salinity of 22).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare culture media at specified salinities according to Walne (1970).

steps.description: Before the start of the batch experiment, pre-cultures (one biological replicate for each condition) were grown semi-continuously for 14 d at either low or high salinity and low or high irradiance (LSLI, HSLI, LSHI, and HSHI), to acclimate the cultures to their respective conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Acclimate microalgal pre-cultures to experimental salinity and irradiance conditions for 14 days.
inputs: Continuous illumination (50 or 200 µmol photons m−2 s−1)

steps.description: Pre-cultures were kept in exponential phase by maintaining an optical density (OD 750 ) between 0.15 and 0.50, by the addition of fresh medium, successively added until volumes reached 260 mL. Thereafter, half of the culture volume was replaced with fresh medium daily or every other day.
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain pre-cultures in exponential phase by monitoring OD750 and adding/replacing fresh medium.

steps.description: After the 2-week acclimation period, the batch experiment was started. Biomass from each pre-culture was distributed into two sterile glass tubes to obtain two biological replicates, and each diluted with fresh medium to yield a starting OD and volume of ∼0.15 and 260 mL, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Start batch experiment by inoculating replicate cultures from acclimated pre-cultures to a target starting OD and volume.

steps.description: Cultures were then grown until Day 5 of the stationary phase.
steps.searchable_summary: Grow batch cultures until Day 5 of the stationary phase.
outputs: Batch cultures at different growth phases (exponential, stationary)

steps.description: all cultures were sampled daily for OD and maximum quantum yield (QY)
steps.searchable_summary: Perform daily sampling of batch cultures for optical density and maximum quantum yield measurements.

steps.description: and on Day 1 in exponential phase (e) and Days 3 and 5 in stationary phase (s1 and s2, respectively) for DW and FA analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform periodic sampling of batch cultures at specific growth phases for dry weight and fatty acid analysis.

steps.description: Additionally, cultures were sampled for nutrient analysis of the media at the start of the experiment, and on the DW and FA sampling days.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform periodic sampling of culture media for nutrient analysis.

methods.description: Calculation of relative growth rates during pre-cultivation based on changes in optical density (attenuation) using a specified equation.

steps.description: Relative growth rates between repeated dilutions during precultivation were calculated according to the changes in attenuation with Equation (1). N x0 and N x are OD 750 after dilution (t x0 ) and before the subsequent dilution (t x ), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative growth rate from sequential optical density measurements (OD750) and time points during pre-cultivation.

methods.description: Measurement of optical density of culture samples using a spectrophotometer at 750 nm, including sample dilution if necessary.

steps.description: Optical density measurements were performed using a spectrophotometer (UV1800, Shimadzu Corporation, Kyoto, Japan) at 750 nm
steps.searchable_summary: Measure optical density of culture samples using a spectrophotometer at 750 nm.

steps.description: and if required, samples were diluted to give an attenuation between 0.2 and 0.8.
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute culture samples to bring optical density within the optimal measurement range (0.2-0.8 attenuation).
inputs: Culture samples with OD outside range

methods.description: Measurement of the maximum quantum yield of photosystem II using an AquaPen after dark incubation.

steps.description: after initial dark incubation between 10 and 60 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Dark-acclimate culture samples for 10 to 60 minutes.

steps.description: The QY was measured with AquaPen (AquaPen-C, AP-C 100, Photon System Instruments, Brno, Czech Republic)
steps.searchable_summary: Measure maximum quantum yield using an AquaPen fluorometer.

methods.description: Determination of the dry weight of biomass from culture samples in triplicates using a method described by Zhu and Lee (1997).

steps.description: DWs (expressed as weight of the dried biomass [g] per volume [L]) were determined in triplicates as described by Zhu and Lee (1997), with 0.5 M ammonium formate as a washing buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine dry weight of biomass from culture samples in triplicates using the method by Zhu and Lee (1997) with ammonium formate wash.

methods.method_name: Fatty Acid Extraction and Derivatization (to FAME)
methods.description: Procedure for extracting fatty acids from microalgal biomass and converting them to fatty acid methyl esters (FAME) via direct esterification for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: For FA analysis, triplicate 10 mL microalgal cultures were harvested by centrifugation (6 min at 2,264 × g) into glass tubes (PYREX),
steps.searchable_summary: Harvest microalgal biomass from triplicate 10 mL culture samples by centrifugation.

steps.description: the supernatant discarded,
steps.searchable_summary: Discard the supernatant after centrifugation.

steps.description: and the pellet covered in nitrogen atmosphere and stored at −20 • C until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Store biomass pellets under nitrogen atmosphere at -20°C.

steps.description: The pellet was dried in the 10 mL tube by evaporating the remaining water under a nitrogen stream,
steps.searchable_summary: Dry the biomass pellet by evaporating residual water under a nitrogen stream.

steps.description: and 18 or 34 µg internal standard (23:0 FAME dissolved in isooctane) was added to exponential or stationary phase samples, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Add internal standard (23:0 FAME in isooctane) to the dried pellet, adjusting amount based on growth phase.

steps.description: The solvent was evaporated under nitrogen stream
steps.searchable_summary: Evaporate the isooctane solvent from the internal standard addition under nitrogen stream.
inputs: Biomass pellet with internal standard in isooctane
outputs: Biomass pellet with dried internal standard

steps.description: and 0.5 mL methylation reagent (2M HCl in methanol) was added.
steps.searchable_summary: Add methylation reagent (2M HCl in methanol) to the sample.
inputs: 0.5 mL 2M HCl in methanol methylation reagent

steps.description: Samples were covered with nitrogen gas, sealed
steps.searchable_summary: Cover samples with nitrogen gas and seal the tubes.

steps.description: and incubated at 90 • C for 2 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate sealed samples at 90°C for 2 hours for direct esterification.

steps.description: After cooling to room temperature,
steps.searchable_summary: Cool samples to room temperature after incubation.

steps.description: half of the methylation reagent was evaporated,
steps.searchable_summary: Evaporate half of the methylation reagent.
inputs: Cooled sample tubes with methylation reagent
outputs: Sample tubes with reduced methylation reagent volume

steps.description: 0.5 mL water was added,
steps.searchable_summary: Add 0.5 mL water to the sample.

steps.description: and the samples were extracted twice with 1 mL isooctane.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract FAMEs from the sample mixture by adding 1 mL isooctane and repeating the extraction twice.

steps.description: The combined extracts of stationary phase samples were diluted with isooctane (1:1, v:v) to yield a final internal standard concentration of approximately 18 µg mL −1 (Steinrücken et al., 2017).
steps.searchable_summary: Combine isooctane extracts and dilute stationary phase samples 1:1 with isooctane to achieve a target internal standard concentration.
outputs: Diluted isooctane extract (for stationary phase samples)

methods.description: Analysis of fatty acid methyl esters (FAMEs) using Gas Chromatography with Flame Ionization Detection (GC-FID) with specific instrument parameters and temperature program.

steps.description: FAMEs were analyzed by GC (7890 gas chromatograph, Agilent, Santa Clara, CA, USA) equipped with an autosampler, split-splitless injector, flame ionization detector (FID), and a 60 m BPX70 capillary column (SGE, Ringwood, Australia) with internal diameter of 0.25 mm and film thickness of 0.25 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze FAME samples using GC-FID with Agilent 7890, split-splitless injector, FID, and SGE BPX70 column.

steps.description: One microliters of sample volumes were injected splitless at 60 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: Inject 1 µL of FAME sample using splitless injection at 60°C.

steps.description: This temperature was maintained for 3 min before raised by 40 • C min −1 to 150 • C and by 1.5 • C min −1 to 230 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: Run GC oven temperature program: hold at 60°C for 3 min, ramp to 150°C at 40°C/min, then ramp to 230°C at 1.5°C/min.

steps.description: Helium, with an estimated average velocity of 30 cm s −1 was used as carrier gas in constant flow mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Set GC carrier gas (Helium) to constant flow mode with an average velocity of 30 cm/s.

steps.description: Injector and detector temperatures were 250 and 300 • C, respectively (Prestegard et al., 2015).
steps.searchable_summary: Set GC injector temperature to 250°C and FID detector temperature to 300°C.

methods.description: Identification of fatty acid methyl esters (FAMEs) using Gas Chromatography coupled to Mass Spectrometry (GC-MS) and comparison with spectral libraries.

steps.description: The FAMEs were identified by analysis on gas chromatography coupled to mass spectrometry (GC-MS) as described in Wasta and Mjøs (2013),
steps.searchable_summary: Identify FAME peaks using GC-MS analysis following the method by Wasta and Mjøs (2013).

steps.description: and by using libraries of mass spectra and retention indices available at www.chrombox. org/data.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify FAME compounds by comparing GC-MS spectra and retention indices to online libraries (e.g., Chrombox).

methods.description: Analysis of dissolved inorganic nutrients (nitrate, nitrite, orthophosphate, silicate) in culture media using colorimetric absorption measurements on an Alpkem-Lab analyzer.

steps.description: For nutrient measurements of the media, 20 mL GF/F filtrates were collected in white plastic vials,
steps.searchable_summary: Collect 20 mL GF/F filtrates of culture media samples in plastic vials.

steps.description: 100 µl chloroform added
steps.searchable_summary: Add 100 µL chloroform as a preservative to media samples.

steps.description: and stored at 4 • C before analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Store preserved media samples at 4°C.

steps.description: Dissolved inorganic nitrate, nitrite, orthophosphate, and silicate were analyzed at the Institute of Marine Research, Bergen, which offers accredited and standardized service for nutrient analyses, using colorimetric absorption measurements on an Alpkem-Lab analyzer (Alpkem Corporation, Oregon USA) according to Parsons et al. (1992).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze dissolved inorganic nutrients (nitrate, nitrite, orthophosphate, silicate) in media samples using colorimetric absorption on an Alpkem-Lab analyzer according to Parsons et al. (1992).
